rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED AL-QARAWI, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD VS. DOMINICAN UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Productions Of Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Thursday, August 1, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF LOREN ESCOBAR's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Productions Of Documents, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. Defendant ELS Educational Services has produced, with its opposition papers, several documents responsive the Plaintiff's document requests at issue in the motion to compel. Defendant's papers also represent that ELS will provide a further response to Document Request 44 (See opposition separate statement at 10:2-4). Nevertheless, Defendant's opposition papers do not reflect that ELS Educational Services has provided Plaintiff Lauren Escobar with verified statements of compliance or inability to comply meeting the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure Sections 2031.220 and 2031.230. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address their positions, on a document request by document request basis, concerning whether the Defendant's document production is complete. Each party is to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL R KURTZMAN et al VS. STEVE SISLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Judgment On The Pleadings With Respect To Pltf'S Verified X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 1, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL KURTZMAN AND MEEHYUN KURTZMAN'S Moiton For Judgment On The Pleadings With Respect To Pltf'S Verified X-Complaint Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address whether failure to file a certificate of merit in accordance with CCP 411.35 can be grounds for a statutory or a common law motion for judgment on the pleadings, taking into account the following: CCP 411.35(g) states that "failure to file a certificate in accordance with this section shall be grounds for a demurrer pursuant to section 430.10 or a motion to strike pursuant to section 435." (emphasis added). CCP 430.10(h) is the "special" demurrer statute regarding certificates of merit. Special demurrers are waived if not timely made. (Weil & Brown, Civil Proc. Before Trial, 7:37.) A motion for judgment on the pleadings has the same function as a general demurrer. (Id., 7:275.) A statutory judgment on the pleadings can be made by a defendant only on the grounds of lack of jurisdiction or failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (See CCP 438(c)(1)(B).) The same limitation exists for common law motions for judgment on the pleadings. The parties should also be prepared to address whether the remedy for moving party at this point is CCP 411.35(h), and whether this subsection also suggests by implication that a motion for judgment on the pleadings does not lie. See generally Price v. Dames & Moore, 92 Cal. App. 4th 355, 361 (2001). In any event, Price states that the failure to file a certificate can ordinarily be cured by amendment. (Id. at 359-360.)=302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: BRIDGEPORT FINANCIAL, INC. VS. SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 1, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT SALLY SWANSON ARCHITECTS Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default And Default Judgment Granted. The Court exercises its equitable power to set aside the default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Olivera v. Grace (1942) 19 Cal. 2d 570, 576. Defendant has shown a meritorious defense (that Plaintiff's assignor Monroe Personnel Services failed to provide services under the agreement with Defendant Sally Swanson Architects and/or that the amount of the default judgment is twice what had allegedly been agreed to and unsupported factually), a satisfactory excuse (defendant's attorney's failure to file an answer and failure to notify defendant of notices of default), and diligence in seeking to set aside the default once it was discovered. Upon learning of the default, defendant promptly sought ex parte relief and then filed this motion on June 21, 2013. Defendant shall file the proposed Answer to the Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 1, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT GORDON & REES LLP, ANDREW CASTRICONE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. CCP 425.16 does not apply to attorney malpractice actions. See PrediWave Corp. v. Simpson (2009) 179 Cal. App. 4th 1204, 1222. Defendant fails to establish that Plaintiff Wilma Baker, Kathleen Burton and Marilyn Snyder were non-clients of defendant. Plaintiffs allege such a relationship in their pleadings, and the parties present conflicting evidence on that issue. Thayer v. Kabateck Brown Kellner LLP (2012) 207 Cal. App. 4th 141, 155 (in ruling on motion, court is to consider the pleadings and supporting and opposing affidavits in determining the nature of the cause of action). In Thayer, it was undisputed that the plaintiff was a non-client and plaintiff did not plead a claim for malpractice; that is not the case here. The Court does not have a copy of page 13 to the Plaintiffs' MPA in Opposition to Motion to Strike filed June 7, 2013. Plaintiff is requested to bring this to the hearing at 9:15 a.m. or, if possible, email a copy of this page to the Court at contestdept302tr@sftc.org as soon as possible. The Court is not inclined to award attorney fees to plaintiffs pursuant to CCP 425.16(c) (1). The motion to strike pursuant to Civil Code 1714.10 is denied because, for the same reason set forth above, defendants have not shown that Plaintiffs Wilma Baker, Kathleen Burton and Marilyn Snyder were non-clients of defendant law firm and that this complaint comes within the ambit of that statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: DGP ASSOCIATES LP VS. AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Entry Of Tentative Ruling On Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For In Camera Review; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant In The Sum Of $7475;
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Thursday, August 1, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DGP ASSOCIATES LP's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For In Camera Review; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant In The Sum Of $7475. (Part 2 of tentative ruling.) The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Within 30 days of service of this order American Guarantee shall produce those portions of the underwriting file previously withheld on the basis of Insurance Code Section 791.13(a). The Section does not apply to information about business entities. It provides in relevant part that "[a]n insurance institution, agent, or insurance-support organization shall not disclose any personal or privileged information about an individual . . ." Ins. Code § 791.13. "Individual" is defined as "any natural person" who fits within a number of categories. Ins.Code 791.02(i). None of the other insureds under the policy fit the definition of "individual." Plaintiff's motion is DENIED with respect to the 29 documents that are direct communications between American Guarantee and its attorneys and the 4 documents involving American Guarantee's direct communications with its attorneys in which one of its consultants was also a party. Pursuant to American Guarantee's request, the court will conduct an in camera review of: (a) the 21 documents involving American Guarantee's internal communications that directly mention communications with its attorneys or directly mention its attorneys' work product; and (b) documents containing reserve information withheld by American Guarantee on the basis of attorney-client privilege and the work product doctrine. Zurich Am. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2007) 155 Cal. App. 4th 1485, 1503 (directing Superior Court to vacate its order compelling production of documents containing reserve information, and to determine on remand, "whether the document contains a discussion of legal advice or strategy of counsel for Zurich" and if so, "whether Zurich waived the privilege by distributing the advice [broadly] within the corporation"). To facilitate the review of documents submitted for in camera inspection, American Guarantee is requested to provide the court with an index of all persons who wrote, sent, or received the documents and a description of their roles in the organization. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of the tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)
